frameworks.name: compositional generalization
frameworks.one_line: transfer learning formalised as recomposing existing functions to solve unseen problems

frameworks.name: Hebbian gating neural network
frameworks.one_line: model in which gating captures curriculum-dependent generalization

frameworks.name: standard neural networks
frameworks.one_line: baseline connectionist models that fail to compositionally generalize like humans

core_question: What determines whether humans can compositionally generalize knowledge to unseen problems?
key_assumptions: transfer is recomposition of existing functions
human compositional ability differs from standard neural nets
manipulations: temporal correlation of items during training
key_findings: humans generalize compositionally where standard neural nets fail
axis-aligned and temporally correlated training improves human generalization
Hebbian-gating model captures human curriculum effects
adults learn composable functions asynchronously with developmental-like discontinuities
interpretations: compositional generalization in humans depends on training structure, not just architecture
asynchronous, discontinuous learning of components resembles child development
